title: Today's AI Brief: The Vatican’s Man Inside Anthropic
summary: A look at the Vatican's involvement in AI and recent developments in the field.
imageAlt: Image from source article for The Vatican’s Man Inside Anthropic

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's AI Brief. I've been digging into some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. Let's start with a surprising connection between the Vatican and AI research.

First up, we have a fascinating story about Chris Olah, an atheist researcher who recently spoke at a ceremony following Pope Leo's historic AI encyclical. The Pope called for 'disarming' AI technology, which is quite a bold statement. What's interesting here is that Olah is now working with Anthropic, a leading AI research organization, and has a seat at the table with the Vatican. This unexpected intersection of faith and technology is definitely worth exploring. It makes you wonder what kind of conversations are happening behind the scenes.

Next, I came across Google's I/O 2026 conference, where they shared updates on making AI more helpful. It seems they're focusing on improving AI's practical applications, which could have a significant impact on how we interact with technology. No major breakthroughs announced, but it's always interesting to see how Google is pushing the boundaries of AI. And who knows, maybe we'll see some of these updates in action soon.

Another news item that caught my eye is the ongoing debate about AI's potential impact on society. While some experts are sounding the alarm, others are more optimistic about the benefits AI could bring. It's clear that AI is a complex issue, and there's no one-size-fits-all solution.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.

sources.title: AI Rings on Fingers Can Interpret Sign Language
